macro_goal: Drop belly fat, fix the left hip asymmetry, and rebuild shock absorption for pain-free running.
week_goal: Introduce unilateral leg movements and separate dynamic warm-ups from static stretching.

exercises.name: Dynamic 90/90 Hip Transitions
exercises.notes: Sit in the 90/90 position. Without using your hands, slowly flip your knees to the other side. 10 reps total to lubricate the hip joints.

exercises.name: Goblet Squats
exercises.notes: Target: 24kg Dumbbell. Holding the weight at your chest forces your torso upright, completely protecting your lower back compared to a barbell.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.notes: Target: 10kg DBs in each hand. Put your rear foot on a bench. This will brutally expose and fix the tight left hip.

exercises.name: Leg Curl Machine
exercises.notes: Target: 140kg. Strict form, no swinging.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carries
exercises.notes: Target: 24kg DB. Hold one heavy dumbbell in your right hand and walk 20 meters. Switch hands. This forces your core to stop your spine from bending, which translates directly to running stability.

exercises.name: Arm Circles & Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.notes: Dynamic warm-up for the shoulder joints. 20 reps each.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Target: 20kg DBs. Dumbbells allow a deeper stretch for the chest than a barbell.

exercises.name: Standing DB Military Press
exercises.notes: Target: 15kg DBs. Standing forces the core to stabilize the weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Chest Flyes
exercises.notes: Target: 10kg DBs. Slight bend in the elbow, stretch the chest.

exercises.name: Tricep Rope Pushdowns
exercises.notes: Target: 32kg on the cable machine. Pull the rope apart at the bottom.

exercises.name: Deep Static Mobility Flow
exercises.notes:
1. Pigeon Pose (2 mins per side)
2. Piriformis Stretch (1 min per side)
3. Supine Twist (1 min per side)
4. Deep 90/90 Hold leaning forward (1 min per side)

exercises.name: Front Crawl Swim
exercises.notes: Smooth, rhythmic cardio to elevate heart rate and burn calories without spinal compression.

exercises.name: Post-Swim Sauna
exercises.notes: 15 Minutes in the sauna.

exercises.name: Lat Pull Downs
exercises.notes: Target: 36kg. Drive the elbows down into your back pockets.

exercises.name: Seated Row
exercises.notes: Target: 36kg. Squeeze the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pulls
exercises.notes: Target: 15kg. Crucial for fixing rounded shoulders and posture.

exercises.name: DB Bicep Curls
exercises.notes: Target: 15kg DBs.

exercises.name: Dynamic Leg Swings
exercises.notes: 15 swings forward/backward, 15 swings side-to-side per leg.

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDLs
exercises.notes: Target: 15kg DB in one hand. Hinge at the hip, keeping the back flat. This will expose tight hamstrings.

exercises.name: Row Machine
exercises.notes: Full-body warm up.

exercises.name: Strava Run Test
exercises.notes: Focus on a mid-foot strike, taking slightly shorter, quicker steps to reduce impact.

exercises.name: Dynamic 90/90 Hip Transitions
exercises.note: 90/90, cannot get both cheeks on floor, struggled a lot more when rolling onto my left leg

exercises.name: Goblet Squats
exercises.note: Goblet Squat 16kg, really felt in my back

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.note: On Bulgarian split squat, started our with 12.5kg in each hadn but really struggled with balance and my lower back is aching, gone to 5kg and I can now success do 8 reps, definitely harder on my right foot as I tend to sway right out of balance

exercises.name: Leg Curl Machine
exercises.note: On Leg curl, we also have prone leg curl. But I did seated leg curl and reduced the weight to 57kg, back really kills after this, from all previous exercises I presume

exercises.name: Suitcase Carries
exercises.note: Suitcase carrys 25kg, abit worried about feet placement

completed_workouts.notes:
Notes on Tuesday
17.5kg on Dumbbell Bench press
Standing DB 10kg 8 reps
Butterfly DB press 10kg 12 reps
Tricep pull down 14.7kg 12 reps
Exercises were too little
